events.title: Bondi terror attack took place amid global spike in foiled IS-inspired plots, researchers find
events.description: Australian counterterrorism experts observe December shooting came amid years of messaging from terror group urging followers to act ‘on their own initiative’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Bondi beach terror attack occurred amid an international spike of anti-western plots in December last year that appeared to be “inspired or instigated” by Islamic State, a new report has found, with many of them targeting holiday events such as Christmas markets. In research published by the West Point Combating Terrorism Center in its publication Sentinel on Thursday , Australian counterterrorism experts Andrew Zammit and Levi West examined Islamic State’s strategic shifts and jihadi tactics in Australia prior to the alleged antisemitic terrorist attack. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Bondi terror attack took place amid global spike in foiled IS-inspired plots, researchers find

events.title: World News in Brief: Airstrikes continue in Ukraine, refugees return to DR Congo, emergency aid for the ‘Dry Corridor’
events.description: The latest wave of Russian attacks across Ukraine have reportedly killed and injured civilians and damaged critical infrastructure in Dnipro, Odesa, Poltava, Zaporizhzhia and other regions.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Airstrikes continue in Ukraine, refugees return to DR Congo, emergency aid for the ‘Dry Corridor’

events.title: LaGuardia pilots raised safety alarms months before deadly runway crash
events.description: Nasa reports show repeated warnings of close calls before crash that killed two pilots and injured 41 others Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Pilot safety concerns about New York ’s LaGuardia airport were filed to aviation officials months before Sunday’s collision between an airplane and a fire truck left two pilots dead and 41 other people hospitalized. According to the aviation safety reporting system administered by the US space agency Nasa, a pilot using the airport in the summer wrote, “Please do something,” after air traffic controllers failed to provide appropriate guidance about multiple nearby aircraft. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ LaGuardia pilots raised safety alarms months before deadly runway crash

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 23 March: Civilians bear brunt of ongoing war
events.description: The war in the Middle East is well into its fourth week and the humanitarian emergency it has sparked continues to be the focus of international attention, along with the energy crisis caused by attacks on shipping in key Strait of Hormuz and other oil and gas infrastructure in the Gulf region. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 23 March: Civilians bear brunt of ongoing war

events.title: Philippines declares ‘national energy emergency’ and boosts coal power as Iran war grinds on
events.description: President’s declaration allows officials to tackle fuel hoarding or profiteering, while energy secretary says country will lean more heavily on coal Middle East crisis – live updates The Philippines president, Ferdinand Marcos, has declared a state of “national energy emergency” as a result of the Middle East war , which his administration said posed “an imminent danger of a critically low energy supply”. The state of emergency, which will initially last for a year, was declared just hours after the country’s energy secretary said the Philippines planned to boost the output of its coal-fired power plants to keep electricity costs down as the war wreaks havoc with gas shipments. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Philippines declares ‘national energy emergency’ and boosts coal power as Iran war grinds on

events.title: Australian growth forecasts slashed as global economy faces inflation spike
events.description: OECD says the Middle East war will test the world’s resilience with Australia expected to suffer from higher rates and inflation Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The world economy is on the brink of a major inflationary spike as soaring fuel prices threaten growth in European and Asian nations, the OECD has warned, and local economists are slashing Australia’s growth prospects for this year and the next amid the ongoing US-Israel attack on Iran. The Organisation for Economic Cooperation and Development’s latest interim outlook said the US-Israel war on Iran will “test the resilience of the global economy”, and warned of the “significant downside risk” to their forecasts should the oil supply disruptions prove more persistent and push energy prices even higher. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australian growth forecasts slashed as global economy faces inflation spike

events.title: Global efforts intensify to secure Strait of Hormuz, avert oil crisis
events.description: For more than 2 weeks, maritime traffic throughout the Strait of Hormuz has been disrupted. Several vessels have been attacked, and the blockage of the world's most strategically important maritime corridors has not just regional, but global repercussions. In an attempt to prevent a major oil crisis and plunging the global economy, countries around the world are trying to find ways to reopen the strait.

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Ukraine in spotlight as Russian strikes surge
events.description: The Security Council is met for an open briefing on Ukraine, requested by European members following Kyiv’s 18 March letter citing a surge in Russian strikes. Inside the country, attacks continue to take a heavy toll: since 19 March, at least 25 civilians have reportedly been killed and more than 130 injured, including children, particularly in Donetsk, Sumy and Zaporizhzhia. We’ll have live coverage below, app users can follow here – and for all our key meetings coverage each day, go here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Ukraine in spotlight as Russian strikes surge

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 24 March: West Bank attacks and Lebanon in focus as Security Council meets
events.description: As the war continues to roil the Middle East and compound suffering for civilians across the region, the economic ramifications of the emergency are still playing out, with the Strait of Hormuz the focus of global attention with crude oil prices surging over $100 a barrel again. Meanwhile, settler attacks have escalated dramatically against Palestinians in the occupied West Bank, a topic that we'll be across today also, with aid updates and live reporting from the Security Council. UN News app users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 24 March: West Bank attacks and Lebanon in focus as Security Council meets

events.title: Alleged narco-terrorists killed as US forces strike suspected drug-trafficking vessel in Caribbean
events.description: SOUTHCOM says Joint Task Force Southern Spear conducted a lethal strike in the Caribbean on March 25, killing four narco-terrorists on a vessel along known drug-trafficking routes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Alleged narco-terrorists killed as US forces strike suspected drug-trafficking vessel in Caribbean

events.title: Iran war deflects attention from Ukraine as Russia starts spring offensive
events.description: The Iran war has deflected global attention from Russia’s all-out invasion of its neighbor Ukraine as Europe’s biggest conflict since World War II enters its fifth year and an emboldened Kremlin undertakes a spring offensive
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war deflects attention from Ukraine as Russia starts spring offensive

events.title: Iran conflict tests Pakistan amid own border clashes as Islamabad touted as venue for US-Tehran talks
events.description: Pakistan, the only nuclear-armed Muslim state, faces mounting pressure as its Saudi defense pact and Iran ties clash amid the intensifying regional war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran conflict tests Pakistan amid own border clashes as Islamabad touted as venue for US-Tehran talks

events.title: Two drone strikes on civilian targets kill 28 people in Sudan
events.description: Market in North Darfur and truck carrying civilians in North Kordofan hit as civil war approaches fourth year At least 28 civilians have been killed in two separate drone strikes in Sudan, according to health workers, as the country’s brutal civil war between the army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) approaches its fourth year . A strike hit a market in the town of Saraf Omra, in North Darfur state, on Wednesday, killing “22 people, including an infant, and injuring 17 more”, a health worker at the local clinic told Agence France-Presse (AFP). Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Two drone strikes on civilian targets kill 28 people in Sudan

events.title: Australian petrol stations report 25% surge in demand as governments plan for ‘biggest energy crisis in history’
events.description: Rationing is not under consideration yet as hundreds of retailers report being without one or more types of fuel Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Petrol stations are reporting a surge in demand of up to 25% in the last fortnight alone on top of already major spikes earlier in the Iran war as Anthony Albanese comes under pressure to devise a national plan to cushion Australia against the “biggest energy crisis in history”. The Australasian Convenience and Petroleum Marketers Association has revealed the scale of the demand on retailers, which has left hundreds of stations across the country without one or more types of fuel. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australian petrol stations report 25% surge in demand as governments plan for ‘biggest energy crisis in history’

events.title: Germany warns of world economic ‘catastrophe’; OECD cuts UK growth forecast
events.description: European powers and bodies warn of economic collapse as the war on Iran nears one-month mark.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Germany warns of world economic ‘catastrophe’; OECD cuts UK growth forecast

events.title: Iran, US harden positions as Tehran tightens grip on Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran and the United States appeared at an impasse Thursday, with each side hardening its position over talks and setting the stage for another potential escalation in the Middle East war. Thousands more US troops neared the region, while Tehran tightened its grip on the crucial Strait of Hormuz. Sirens over Israel warned of barrages of incoming Iranian missiles, and Gulf nations worked to intercept fire. Heavy strikes were reported in Iran’s capital and other cities.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran, US harden positions as Tehran tightens grip on Strait of Hormuz

events.title: World News in Brief: 273 million out of school, deadly attacks on Ukraine, migratory species in danger
events.description: The number of children and young people out of school worldwide has climbed for the seventh consecutive year, reaching 273 million, according to a new report from the UN education agency, UNESCO.

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 25 March: All eyes on Strait of Hormuz; war is ‘out of control’, UN chief warns
events.description: It’s day 26 of war in the Middle East. The UN chief says with the conflict now totally out of control, diplomacy must prevail. Ongoing strikes in Israel and Iran have included intensifying Israeli attacks against Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, while some US troops are heading to the region, the Pentagon has confirmed. Meanwhile, Iran has told the UN maritime agency that the Strait of Hormuz remains open to “non-hostile” ships not associated with the US and Israel. UN News app users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 25 March: All eyes on Strait of Hormuz; war is ‘out of control’, UN chief warns

events.title: Southeast Asia turns to nuclear as Iran war disrupts energy supplies
events.description: Analysts say the Iran war energy crisis is also adding momentum to nuclear interest and action in the region.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Southeast Asia turns to nuclear as Iran war disrupts energy supplies

events.title: Iraq caught between US and Iran, facing attacks from both warring sides & Iran-aligned actors within
events.description: Oliver Farry is pleased to welcome Hayder Al-Shakeri, Research Fellow with the MENA programme at Chatham House. Al-Shakeri describes Iraq as a state navigating a precarious balance between two powerful allies whose conflict is increasingly unfolding on its own territory. This position is not merely diplomatic but deeply structural, rooted in economic dependence, security arrangements, and geographic realities that bind Iraq simultaneously to both Washington and Tehran. The lack of strong institutions, and a fully-functioning central government, constrains the state’s ability to respond.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iraq caught between US and Iran, facing attacks from both warring sides & Iran-aligned actors within

events.title: War in the Mideast: Iranian cluster ballistic missiles test Israel’s defences
events.description: As the regional conflict between Israel and Iran enters a volatile new phase, a banned weapon of war is reappearing over civilian centers. While Israel’s defence systems remain robust, a new threat – the cluster ballistic missile – is testing the limits of safety. Unlike a single warhead, these missiles release hundreds of "bomblets," turning neighborhoods into active minefields long after the sirens stop. FRANCE 24’s Olivia Bizot reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War in the Mideast: Iranian cluster ballistic missiles test Israel’s defences

events.title: Floods in Kenya leave 88 dead and thousands displaced
events.description: The number killed in floods in Kenya has risen to 88, the government has said, as two rivers burst their banks causing more displacement.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Floods in Kenya leave 88 dead and thousands displaced

events.title: Dire fertiliser shortage a lurking threat due to Hormuz crisis
events.description: Since the start of the Middle East conflict with Israeli and US strikes on Iran on 28 February, concerns have been growing over rising oil and commodity prices.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Dire fertiliser shortage a lurking threat due to Hormuz crisis

events.title: US strikes against Iran-backed militias in Iraq reportedly continue as Baghdad warns of 'right to respond'
events.description: Iraq's Prime Minister al-Sudani threatened to confront and respond to U.S. airstrikes on PMF headquarters, calling the attacks violations of Iraqi sovereignty.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US strikes against Iran-backed militias in Iraq reportedly continue as Baghdad warns of 'right to respond'

events.title: Watch: Inside Palestinian homes attacked by settlers
events.description: Violence began after a teenage settler was killed, reportedly after being hit by a vehicle driven by a Palestinian.

events.title: UN chief calls for immediate release of 118 detained UN staff
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres has called for the immediate release of the 118 UN staff currently detained worldwide as attacks against the United Nations increase with 179 personnel arrested or detained last year alone.

events.title: Nigeria takes its place on world stage in quest to become regional superpower
events.description: Nigeria and UK look to strengthen trade and economic ties amid growing calls from Africa and Caribbean for reparative justice “There are chapters in our shared history that I know have left some painful marks,” King Charles said during a state banquet to welcome the Nigerian president, Bola Tinubu, to the UK, in a year in which the monarch is expected to come under renewed pressure to make a formal apology for transatlantic slavery and colonialism. But while demands grow from African and Caribbean nations for the UK to further reparative justice, Nigeria and the UK are looking to the future of global trade. Continue reading...

events.title: UN calls for solidarity and political will to stamp out racial discrimination
events.description: Nearly 70 years after South African police fired upon peaceful demonstrators in Sharpeville protesting apartheid-era laws, killing 69, the UN renewed the commitment to work for justice and equality on Monday, marking the International Day for the Elimination of Racial Discrimination.

events.title: Author Barry Hoffner on overcoming grief by travelling to every country on Earth
events.description: A man who suffered extreme grief after the sudden death of his wife has spoken to FRANCE 24 about his project to travel to every single country in the world, and how it brought him out of his grief. Barry Hoffner first took one trip and found that his reconnection with people was helping him to heal. He discovered a way of shifting from grief to the idea that there was something left in life. His book is entitled "Belonging to the World: A Journey from Grief to Connection in Every Country on Earth". He spoke to us in Perspective.

events.title: Arizona gun dealer armed two Mexican drug cartels, DOJ says
events.description: An Arizona gun dealer was indicted on charges of allegedly providing weapons to the CJNG and Sinaloa Cartel, both designated foreign terrorist organizations.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Arizona gun dealer armed two Mexican drug cartels, DOJ says

events.title: The U.S. and Iran are in 'indirect talks,' says intermediary Pakistan, as war rages on
events.description: Pakistan's foreign minister said the country is relaying messages and that Iran is deliberating on a U.S. proposal. Israel says it killed the Iranian Revolutionary Guard's navy chief.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 The U.S. and Iran are in 'indirect talks,' says intermediary Pakistan, as war rages on

events.title: In Lebanon, paramedics mourn their own killed in Israeli strike
events.description: Wearing identical uniforms, a dozen paramedics ​gathered around a pair of caskets in the southern Lebanese town of Nabatieh as they prepared to bury two of their own killed in an Israeli strike. Ali Jaber and Joud Sleiman were hit on Tuesday as they headed out on a motorcycle ​in southern Lebanon ‌on a rescue mission, according to Lebanon's health ministry. Both were wearing paramedic uniforms and their motorcycle was ⁠clearly marked as an ambulance complete with flashing lights.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 In Lebanon, paramedics mourn their own killed in Israeli strike

events.title: Iranian naval chief killed in attack, Israel says
events.description: Israel says it has killed IRGC naval commander Alireza Tangsiri in a strike near Bandar Abbas by the Strait of Hormuz.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iranian naval chief killed in attack, Israel says

events.title: Sudan: Hospital strike highlights surge in drone attacks on civilians
events.description: The death toll from a horrific attack on a hospital in Sudan’s Darfur has risen further, amid a “sharp increase” in drone attacks against civilians this year, UN agencies said on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan: Hospital strike highlights surge in drone attacks on civilians

events.title: War in the Middle East: Cultural treasures damaged, with more at risk
events.description: Since the outbreak of war on 28 February, several unique sites of cultural significance have been damaged in Iran, Israel and Lebanon, alongside immense suffering, displacement and death.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War in the Middle East: Cultural treasures damaged, with more at risk

events.title: US troops brace for ‘hit-and-run’ guerilla attacks as 82nd Airborne deploys to Iran, military analyst warns
events.description: The 82nd Airborne Division deployment to the Middle East is intended to pressure Iran into accepting U.S. ceasefire terms, military analyst Michael Eisenstadt says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US troops brace for ‘hit-and-run’ guerilla attacks as 82nd Airborne deploys to Iran, military analyst warns

events.title: Trump, Starmer agree Strait of Hormuz must reopen as Middle East conflict escalates
events.description: Strait of Hormuz closure dominates a Trump-Starmer call, as Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to Iran warns of further U.S. strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump, Starmer agree Strait of Hormuz must reopen as Middle East conflict escalates

events.title: REPLAY: NATO Secretary General annual report for 2025
events.description: NATO's European allies and Canada increased defence spending by 20% ​in 2025 compared to the previous year in real terms, alliance chief Mark Rutte said in his annual report published Thursday, urging NATO members to keep up ​the momentum. "I ‌expect Allies at the next NATO Summit in ⁠Ankara to show they are on a clear and credible path towards the 5% objective," he wrote, ‌adding that "a strong transatlantic bond remains essential in an age of ⁠global uncertainty".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 REPLAY: NATO Secretary General annual report for 2025

events.title: Militants in northern Nigeria kill 10 security forces and 1 resident, officials say
events.description: Authorities in northern Nigeria say armed militants ambushed security forces who were responding to a distress call in the northwestern state of Kebbi, fatally shooting nine soldiers, a police officer and one resident
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Militants in northern Nigeria kill 10 security forces and 1 resident, officials say

events.title: Suspect in fatal New Jersey hit-and-run crash is illegal alien, fugitive: DHS
events.description: Wilson Morocho-Necta, 33, is charged in a fatal hit-and-run in Morristown, NJ, as a federal source identifies him as an illegal alien from Ecuador.

events.title: 'Americas Counter Cartel Coalition': Inside the US strategy to combat narco terror, confront China, other foes
events.description: Joseph M. Humire, acting assistant secretary of War for Homeland Defense and Americas Security Affairs, explains the Trump administration's strategy for the Western Hemisphere.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Americas Counter Cartel Coalition': Inside the US strategy to combat narco terror, confront China, other foes

events.title: UK counterterrorism police probe antisemitic arson attack as Iran-linked group claims responsibility
events.description: An Iran-linked group claims responsibility for torching Jewish ambulances outside a Golders Green synagogue in London as counterterrorism police investigate.
events.ticker_label: 💥 UK counterterrorism police probe antisemitic arson attack as Iran-linked group claims responsibility

events.title: India news: Andhra Pradesh bus fire leaves 13 dead
events.description: A bus burst into flames after colliding with a truck in India's Andhra Pradesh. At least 13 people have died and 20 others are injured. DW has the latest.
events.ticker_label: 💥 India news: Andhra Pradesh bus fire leaves 13 dead

events.title: A city opens its doors to the world: New York and the United Nations
events.description: Born from the ashes of the Second World War, the dream of a more peaceful and fairer world drew nations together in 1945 under a new vision for humanity: the United Nations.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 A city opens its doors to the world: New York and the United Nations

events.title: Revolutionary Tourism: Inside the $600M marriage of dark money and far-left agitprop
events.description: A Fox News Digital investigation shows how a network tied to Neville Roy Singham turns activism into coordinated global protest movements — from Cuba to U.S. cities — using a repeatable playbook of messaging, mobilization and media amplification.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Revolutionary Tourism: Inside the $600M marriage of dark money and far-left agitprop

events.title: UN weather agency warns of record ‘climate imbalance’ as planetary warming accelerates
events.description: All-time high greenhouse gas concentrations in Earth’s atmosphere continue to drive heat records on land and sea, with long-lasting consequences for humanity, the World Meteorological Organization (WMO) warned on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UN weather agency warns of record ‘climate imbalance’ as planetary warming accelerates

events.title: NATO scrambles jets as Russia fires nearly 400 drones toward Ukraine, signaling new spring offensive
events.description: Russia launched nearly 400 drones and missiles at Ukraine, prompting Poland and Romania to scramble NATO fighter jets as strikes neared their airspace.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 NATO scrambles jets as Russia fires nearly 400 drones toward Ukraine, signaling new spring offensive

events.title: Is Europe heading to an energy crisis?
events.description: Europe is bracing for a supply crunch and a price shock as the Iran conflict drags on.

events.title: Iran to Lebanon: Four million people displaced by US-Israeli war
events.description: Three million Iranians and at least a million Lebanese have been forced from their homes because of US-Israeli attacks.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran to Lebanon: Four million people displaced by US-Israeli war

events.title: Iranian naval commander Alireza Tangsiri killed in attack, Israel says
events.description: Defence minister claims Israel has killed Alireza Tangsiri, citing his role in blocking the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Video: Storms wash away vehicles and flood homes across Middle East
events.description: Vehicles were washed away in floods in Oman and homes were flooded in Syria as storms brought floods across the region.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Video: Storms wash away vehicles and flood homes across Middle East

events.title: Mark Carney rebukes Air Canada chief over English-only crash message
events.description: The prime minister says the condolence video after the fatal LaGuardia crash revived anger over linguistic rights Canada’s prime minister, Mark Carney , has said a decision by Air Canada’s top executive to post an English-only message of condolence after a deadly crash in New York showed a “lack of judgment, a lack of compassion”. Amid growing calls for his resignation, the airline chief’s misstep has once again revived frustrations and fears over linguistic rights protections in the province of Quebec, where French is the only official language. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Mark Carney rebukes Air Canada chief over English-only crash message

events.title: Trump’s trip to meet Xi Jinping in China rescheduled for May due to Iran war
events.description: US president says he will host Chinese leader in a reciprocal visit later this year Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump will meet Xi Jinping in May during the US president’s first visit to China in eight years, a closely watched trip that had been postponed due to the Iran war. Trump was initially slated to travel next week, but will now visit Beijing on 14 and 15 May, he wrote in a post on Truth Social on Wednesday. Trump said he would host the Chinese leader in a reciprocal visit in Washington later this year. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump’s trip to meet Xi Jinping in China rescheduled for May due to Iran war

events.title: US war in Iran: China casts itself as savior of SE Asia
events.description: As the US-Israel war with Iran sends fuel prices surging across Southeast Asia, Beijing is using the crisis to position itself as a steadier and more reliable partner than Washington.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US war in Iran: China casts itself as savior of SE Asia

events.title: Iran war: 'Get serious before it is too late,' Trump warns
events.description: US President Donald Trump has voiced frustration with Iran's negotiators, urging them to fully engage while there is still time. Meanwhile, Israel says it has killed the Revolutionary Guard's naval chief. DW has more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: 'Get serious before it is too late,' Trump warns

events.title: Ukraine: danger is only increasing, warns UN human rights office
events.description: More than four years since Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, the “danger is only increasing”, particularly from attack drones, a top UN human rights official warned on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: danger is only increasing, warns UN human rights office

events.title: Missing woman’s sister says Nancy Guthrie case now a ‘hope roller coaster’ for family as search continues
events.description: Julie Murray, whose sister Maura Murray vanished in 2004, warns that urgency fades in these cases as Nancy Guthrie remains missing after 50 days.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Missing woman’s sister says Nancy Guthrie case now a ‘hope roller coaster’ for family as search continues

events.title: Illegal immigrant accused of fatal hit and run that killed North Carolina motorcyclist
events.description: An illegal immigrant is accused in a fatal alleged hit-and-run that killed motorcyclist Christopher Babcock, 62, in North Carolina, and an ICE detainer was filed.

events.title: Olympic women's sport limited to biological females
events.description: The women's category of Olympic sports will be limited to biological females from 2028, says the International Olympic Committee.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Olympic Committee bans transgender women from female events
events.description: The International Olympic Committee has said it will reintroduce genetic gender testing to determine eligibility for female events at the 2028 Los Angeles Games and beyond.

events.title: Russia says it shot down almost 400 Ukrainian drones as Moscow, Kyiv escalate strikes
events.description: Russia is reporting that it has shot down hundreds of Ukrainian drones in a massive overnight attack across its regions and Crimea
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia says it shot down almost 400 Ukrainian drones as Moscow, Kyiv escalate strikes

events.title: Gaza: Commitment to US-backed plan crucial to recovery, Security Council hears
events.description: As tensions escalate in the Middle East, the international community must not lose sight of the situation in Gaza, an official with US President Donald Trump’s Board of Peace across the shattered enclave said on Tuesday in his first appearance in the UN Security Council.

events.title: Zimbabwe says 15 nationals killed after being lured to fight for Russia
events.description: Zimbabwe says it is engaging the Russian government to repatriate bodies and bring home those still alive.

events.title: US military sends drones, alongside 200 troops, to Nigeria amid fears of renewed Boko Haram insurgency
events.description: The U.S. military deployed MQ-9 Reaper drones to Nigeria for intelligence-gathering and training as the African country is grappling with a security crisis.

events.title: North Korean dictator says government will keep cementing nation's 'irreversible status as a nuclear power'
events.description: North Korean figure Kim Jong Un said the government will bolster the nation's "irreversible status as a nuclear power," The Associated Press reported.

events.title: Air Canada CEO 'deeply saddened' his poor French skills distracted from victims of crash
events.description: Michael Rousseau has faced calls to resign for not speaking French in his message after the LaGuardia crash, which killed two Air Canada pilots.

events.title: Teen volunteer paramedic killed in Israeli strike in Lebanon
events.description: This 15-year-old volunteer paramedic was killed alongside a colleague in an Israeli strike on Lebanon.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Teen volunteer paramedic killed in Israeli strike in Lebanon

events.title: Canadian woman held with daughter by ICE warns all immigrants to ‘lie low’
events.description: Tania Warner says she has documents showing she is in the US legally, but immigration agents were not swayed Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A Canadian woman who has been imprisoned with her seven-year-old daughter by Immigration and Customs Enforcement ( ICE ) has cautioned other immigrants that they are at risk of detention, even if they follow the correct legal process – and warned them to keep out of sight for as long as Donald Trump is president. “Don’t go anywhere near a checkpoint, and if your papers are in processing, just lay low. Trump meant what he said – he is trying to get rid of everyone, whether they are good or bad,” said Tania Warner, 47, who is currently held with her autistic daughter, Ayla, at the Dilley immigration processing center in south Texas. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Canadian woman held with daughter by ICE warns all immigrants to ‘lie low’

events.title: ‘They can reach me wherever’: China using financial tactics to coerce people who flee, says report
events.description: UK urged to tackle transnational repression, as dissidents say Beijing has targeted them with tax bills and other threats “I didn’t feel safe, even though I’m not based in Hong Kong any more,” said Christopher Mung Siu-tat after getting tax bills from Hong Kong authorities. “The regime can reach me by their long arms wherever I am.” Siu-tat, the executive director at the Hong Kong Labour Rights Monitor, a UK-based NGO, fled Beijing’s sweeping national security laws years ago. The letters are the latest example of a series of transnational repression (TNR) tactics the 54-year-old has faced in recent years. Continue reading...

events.title: Iran rejects Trump's proposal to end the war and lays out 5 conditions
events.description: Iran says no to President Trump's ceasefire plan and publicized a counterproposal that includes war reparations and Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran rejects Trump's proposal to end the war and lays out 5 conditions

events.title: Israeli judge closes case of Palestinian teen's death in prison despite evidence of starvation
events.description: A newly unsealed sentence from a court ruling reveals that an Israeli judge acknowledged that a Palestinian teen had been starved in Israeli prison but stopped an investigation into the case

events.title: Israel says Iranian leader who ordered Strait of Hormuz closure killed in targeted strike
events.description: Israel has eliminated Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy Commander Alireza Tangsiri in a strike, Israel's Minister of Defense Israel Katz indicated.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel says Iranian leader who ordered Strait of Hormuz closure killed in targeted strike

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: Sweden's top general says U.S. remains 'really important ally'
events.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Sweden's chief of defense, Gen. Michael Claesson, about NATO, the wars in Ukraine and Iran, and Europe's relationship with the United States.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sweden's top general says U.S. remains 'really important ally'

events.title: World Cup 2026: Cost, Trump and travel give fans pause
events.description: As teams battle it out for the final six spots, loyal fans are making plans for World Cup 2026. DW spoke to supporters across the world to hear about five-figure expenditure, visa uncertainty and safety concerns.

events.title: In Hungary, some mayors can reject 'undesirable' residents
events.description: In Hungary, mayors now have the right to "select" residents of their towns after a new law was passed last summer. The far-right mayor of Pilis, a town of 12,000 inhabitants, is one of them. One former resident of the town, who was forced to leave, explains why he believes the procedure is discriminatory, particularly towards the Roma minority. Our France 2 colleagues report, with FRANCE 24's Florent Marchais.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 In Hungary, some mayors can reject 'undesirable' residents

events.title: Italy's Meloni says to boost cooperation with Algeria on gas deliveries
events.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni said during a visit to Algeria Wednesday that the two countries would work together towards "increasing deliveries of Algerian gas" to the EU member state. The two would work together on projects including shale gas extraction and offshore exploration, she said at a press conference alongside Algerian President Abdelmadjid Tebboune.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Italy's Meloni says to boost cooperation with Algeria on gas deliveries

events.title: EU votes in favor of migrant 'return hubs'
events.description: Human rights groups have warned of asylum seekers disappearing into "legal black holes" beyond EU borders, while concerns have also been raised over the influence of the far right over the legislation.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 EU votes in favor of migrant 'return hubs'

events.title: UN pushes emergency plan for Cuba aid amid US talks
events.description: The UN has presented an aid action plan for Cuba to US officials, which even involves fuel tracking models. Meanwhile, former President Raul Castro has reportedly been involved in talks with the US.

events.title: Australia bans visitors from Iran for 6 months amid war
events.description: Australia is concerned Iranian visitors may overstay their visas because of the war in the Middle East.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia bans visitors from Iran for 6 months amid war

events.title: SpaceX reportedly set to launch IPO valuing firm at $1.75 trillion
events.description: Elon Musk's profitable rocket and satellite internet company SpaceX is reportedly preparing to raise $75 billion on public equity markets as its subsidiary xAI burns cash. But first: a pair of US court verdicts against social media giants could signal the sector's Big Tobacco moment, and France's finance minister convenes G7 officials to address the economic fallout from the Iran war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SpaceX reportedly set to launch IPO valuing firm at $1.75 trillion

events.title: Over 90% of Iranian missiles intercepted, but a critical vulnerability is growing, report warns
events.description: Experts say Iran's cheap drones and missiles are depleting allied interceptor stockpiles, raising questions about long-term air defense sustainability.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Over 90% of Iranian missiles intercepted, but a critical vulnerability is growing, report warns

events.title: What happened in the seconds before Air Canada plane crashed at LaGuardia
events.description: BBC Verify breaks down the moments before the deadly collision with a fire truck at the New York airport.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ What happened in the seconds before Air Canada plane crashed at LaGuardia

events.title: How the deadly LaGuardia Airport crash unfolded
events.description: Video shows the aftermath of the collision between a passenger plane and a firefighting vehicle on the runway of the New York airport.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ How the deadly LaGuardia Airport crash unfolded

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Air Canada chief apologizes after English-only message to plane crash victims
events.description: Michael Rousseau says his lack of French overshadowed families’ grief after Mark Carney and Quebec denounced his video Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The chief executive of Air Canada has apologized for his inability to express himself in French after politicians called for his resignation for his English-only message of condolence after Sunday’s deadly crash in New York. Air Canada’s CEO, Michael Rousseau, has been criticized for the four-minute condolence video posted online that included only two French words – “bonjour” and “merci”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Air Canada chief apologizes after English-only message to plane crash victims

events.title: Lead Imagineer on the new 'Frozen' ride at Disneyland Paris
events.description: France is famous for its theme parks: Puy du Fou, Parc Astérix and the one that's the most visited in Europe: Disneyland Paris. The park is about to open the World of Frozen, part of the new Disney Adventure World park. Fans of Elsa, Anna and Olaf are in for a treat! We speak to the creative mind behind the new park, Michel den Dulk, in this edition of Entre Nous.

events.title: Football: Who is behind France's so-called "World Cup song"?
events.description: Ahead of their friendly #football game with #France, all Brazilian supporters are talking about is 'Imbattables', dubbed France's 2026 #WorldCup song.

events.title: France bids farewell to ex-PM Jospin who 'modernised' nation
events.description: France on Thursday bid farewell to former prime minister Lionel Jospin, with President Emmanuel Macron leading a national tribute to the influential left?wing statesman credited with major welfare reforms. Jospin, who introduced the 35-hour work week and civil partnerships for gay couples when he was head of government from 1997 to 2002, died on Sunday aged 88.
events.ticker_label: 💥 France bids farewell to ex-PM Jospin who 'modernised' nation

events.title: Paris neighbourhoods: The iconic 6th arrondissement
events.description: In this edition of French Connections, Genie Godula and Florence Villeminot continue their tour of Paris with a stop in one of the French capital's oldest and most influential neighbourhoods: the 6th. Le sixième arrondissement has quintessential Left Bank (Rive Gauche) energy. It's intellectual, romantic, sophisticated and no doubt a little smug, but definitely worth a stop on any visit to Paris. Real Parisians love to argue over whether it's too touristy, while often secretly adoring it. Join us as we take you on a tour of this iconic and historic district of Paris.

events.title: 'Return hubs': EU parliament clears the way for offshore migrant detention centres
events.description: The European Parliament on Thursday approved a proposed reform that would allow member states to deport migrants whose asylum applications have been rejected to offshore detention facilities outside EU borders. Migrant arrivals to Europe have declined in recent years, shifting the focus to repatriating people ordered to return to their country of origin.

events.title: A knife-wielding man kills a woman at a Tokyo Pokemon shop before taking his own life
events.description: A knife attack at a Pokémon store in downtown Tokyo has left a woman and the attacker dead, police say

events.title: Sarah Mullally celebrated as the Church of England's first female leader, in photos
events.description: Sarah Mullally has been formally installed as archbishop of Canterbury, marking the start of her public ministry as the first woman to lead the Church of England. She will serve as spiritual head of the global Anglican Communion, a network of independent churches with more than 100 million members. The church ordained its first female priests in 1994 and its first female bishop in 2015. This is a photo gallery curated by AP photo editors.

events.title: ‘Truly transformative’ new diagnostic tools can help end tuberculosis
events.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) called on Tuesday for countries to step up action to end tuberculosis (TB) – one of the world’s deadliest infectious killers – by expanding access to new diagnostic tools that can help save lives.

events.title: Prepare for turbulence - how a prolonged Middle East conflict could reshape how we fly

events.title: UN votes to describe slave trade as ‘gravest crime against humanity’
events.description: Members call for reparatory justice as landmark resolution aims for ‘political recognition at the highest level’ The United Nations has voted to describe the transatlantic chattel slave trade as the “gravest crime against humanity” and called for reparations as “a concrete step towards remedying historical wrongs”. The landmark resolution passed on Wednesday was backed by the African Union (AU) and the Caribbean Community (Caricom). It had been proposed by Ghana’s president, John Dramani Mahama , who said: “Let it be recorded that when history beckoned, we did what was right for the memory of millions who suffered the indignity of slavery.” Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UN votes to describe slave trade as ‘gravest crime against humanity’

events.title: Australia refuses to say how many Chinese nationals are arriving by boat, saying it may damage bilateral relations
events.description: Exclusive: Indonesia reports growing number of attempts by Chinese nationals to organise boat journeys, as Australian authorities refuse to reveal details Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Australian government has refused to reveal how many Chinese nationals have arrived in Australia by boat since 2024, saying that disclosing the figure may harm relations with other countries. However, reports by Indonesian police show that there has been a consistent trend of Chinese nationals attempting to reach Australia through Indonesia as an alternative to “zouxian”, or “walking the line” – the illegal migration route through Central America to the US via the Darién Gap between Colombia and Panama. Continue reading...

events.title: Iran rejects U.S. peace plan. And, jury finds Meta, Google to blame in addiction trial

events.title: ​Germany: Building sector warns of spiralling costs

events.title: Jailed Bolsonaro granted ‘humanitarian house arrest’ amid failing health
events.description: Former Brazil president, serving 27 years over attempted coup, given initial 90-day period that could be extended Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Brazil ’s former president Jair Bolsonaro has been granted permission to serve his 27-year sentence for a coup attempt at home instead of in prison because of his failing health. The decision by supreme court justice Alexandre de Moraes followed Bolsonaro’s hospitalization since 13 March for pneumonia, one of several health problems the former leader has faced since he was stabbed by a man in 2018 before he was elected president. Continue reading...

events.title: Syria integration deal: Kurdish fighters begin to integrate state forces
events.description: Syria’s government has begun implementing an agreement with the Kurdish-led Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) to gradually integrate Kurdish fighters and administrative structures into state forces. The move follows weeks of fighting during which government forces retook significant areas in the northeast that had remained under SDF control for over a decade. FRANCE 24's Wassim Nasr tells us more.

events.title: Meet Iran's hardline speaker who threatened to burn US forces — reportedly Tehran's point man for talks
events.description: Iranian parliament speaker Ghalibaf has threatened U.S. forces while reportedly being eyed by the Trump administration as a potential Iran talks channel.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: More young people want to vote in New Zealand’s Māori electorates. What are they and how do they work?
events.description: Growing numbers of young voters are signing up to the Māori electoral roll as debate flares over the need for dedicated seats ahead of November’s election More young people have signed up to vote in Māori electorates, new figures from the electoral commission show, as New Zealand prepares for an election this year. The rise comes after years of tense relations between Indigenous New Zealanders and the centre-right coalition government. The latest figures show 58% of eligible 18- to 24-year-olds have registered for the Māori roll, up from 50% in 2023. Continue reading...

events.title: Australia urged to swap diesel for electric buses as fuel costs soar
events.description: Electric buses are just 1% of the Australian fleet compared with 80% in urban China, a quarter in the Netherlands and 12% in the UK Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Sign up for climate and environment editor Adam Morton’s free Clear Air newsletter here As diesel climbs past $3 a litre amid fuel security concerns, transport advocates are calling for the rollout of electric buses across Australia to be prioritised. In Australia, just 1% of buses are electric, compared with 80% of the urban fleet in China, a quarter in the Netherlands and 12% in the UK. Continue reading...

events.title: NATO reports 20% increase in Europe, Canada's defence spending

US President Donald Trump has repeatedly demanded that NATO partners significantly boost defence expenditures, with leaders agreeing at a summit last year to spend 5 percent ​of GDP on defence and related investments by 2035.

events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Lebo M sues comedian Learnmore Jonasi claiming Circle of Life misrepresentation
events.description: Grammy winner seeks more than $20m in damages over mistranslation of The Lion King chant A Grammy-winning South African composer who wrote and performed the opening chant in Circle of Life for Disney’s The Lion King is suing a comedian for allegedly damaging his reputation by intentionally misrepresenting the song’s meaning on a podcast and in his standup routine. Lebohang Morake’s lawsuit accuses the Zimbabwean comedian Learnmore Mwanyenyeka, known as Learnmore Jonasi, of intentionally mistranslating the chant, which launches the 1994 movie and is central to staged versions as well as Disney’s 2019 remake. Continue reading...

events.title: UN calls for reparations to remedy the 'historical wrongs' of trafficking enslaved Africans
events.description: The U.N. General Assembly adopted a resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans "the gravest crime against humanity" and calling for reparations.

events.title: For the first time in more than 1,400 years, Church of England gets a woman leader
events.description: A new archbishop of Canterbury has been installed in a historic ceremony. Sarah Mullally is the 106th person to hold the job, and the first woman.

events.title: Thousands of U.S. troops deploy to Middle East. And, the latest on DHS funding talks
events.description: The U.S. is sending thousands of paratroopers from the 82nd Airborne to the Middle East. And, congressional Republicans present Democrats with a new deal to fund the Department of Homeland Security.

events.title: 'Looksmaxxing' — the manosphere beauty cult
events.description: The online trend with roots in misogyny encourages socially isolated young men to undertake painful practices to gain beauty and status — and its language is reaching the White House.

events.title: Lufthansa and the role of big business in the Holocaust
events.description: German airline Lufthansa is one of many big-name brands that collaborated with the Nazis during the Holocaust. As the airline celebrates its 100th anniversary, why does its Nazi past continue to fly under the radar?

events.title: Smart sleepers could be the glow-up night trains need
events.description: Europe has a bit of an on-off relationship with night trains. A Berlin team's smart cabin concept could lead to greater commitment.

events.title: UN recognizes slave trade as gravest crime against humanity
events.description: The UN General Assembly has passed the resolution on transatlantic slavery despite opposition from the US and European countries.

events.title: France: Islamic scholar Tariq Ramadan gets 18 years for rape
events.description: A Paris court has sentenced in absentia Islamic scholar Tariq Ramadan for the rape of three women between 2009 and 2016. The verdict marks one of the most high-profile cases linked to the "Me Too" movement.

events.title: Humans and dogs — scientists find new proof of ancient bond
events.description: Dogs were our friends and guardians thousands of years before the end of the last ice age, with new studies identifying a puppy that lived 15,800 years ago.

events.title: Go to sleep in France, wake up in Germany: Paris-Berlin night train restarts
events.description: Go to sleep in France, wake up in Germany. That's the idea between the iconic Paris-Berlin sleeper train, which is once again being revived. The night train was launched by SNCF in 2023 after a near-decade hiatus… But managed only stayed in operation until December 2025. Now, a new company is taking on the mission.

events.title: Meta and YouTube verdict: Social media shakedown or bellwether?
events.description: PRESS REVIEW – Thursday, March 26: We look at reactions after Meta and YouTube were found guilty of social media harms in what is being hailed as a "landmark" trial. Will it give impetus to thousands of other lawsuits in the waiting? Also: Italy's Giorgia Meloni headed to Algeria in a lightning-quick diplomatic visit aimed at securing gas supplies. Plus: Vogue's publisher Conde Nast goes to court with a canine magazine called... Dogue.

events.title: Gang-rape victim, 25, to be euthanised after father's legal challenge fails
events.description: A paraplegic gang-rape victim said she will wear her "prettiest dress" and hopes to "finally rest" when she is euthanised today.

events.title: Instagram and Facebook owner Meta ordered to pay £280m for knowingly harming children
events.description: Meta, the owner of Facebook, Instagram and Whatsapp, has been ordered to pay $375m (£280m) in damages after it was found to have knowingly harmed children's mental health.

events.title: Skeleton of Three Musketeers' d'Artagnan may have been found under Dutch church
events.description: Remains found under a church floor in the Netherlands could belong to the famous musketeer d'Artagnan.

events.title: The Iranian former commander who could be Trump's pick to negotiate with
events.description: A former commander of Iran's Revolutionary Guards has emerged as a possible US contact for talks - as the new Supreme Leader's condition remains uncertain.

events.title: Wildlife Photographer of the Year: Pick your favourite snap
events.description: The 2026 Wildlife Photographer of the Year has named its People's Choice winner, selected from more than 60,000 entries and voted for by the public.

events.title: Italian politician resigns over stake in mafia-linked restaurant
events.description: Italian justice undersecretary Andrea Delmastro has resigned after it emerged he had held a stake in a restaurant linked to the mafia.

events.title: South Africa arrests 12 senior police officers on suspicion of corruption
events.description: South African prosecutors say anti-graft investigators have arrested 12 senior police officers over alleged corruption and fraud tied to a police services contract
events.ticker_label: 💥 South Africa arrests 12 senior police officers on suspicion of corruption

events.title: Italy's tourism minister resigns under pressure from Meloni
events.description: Italy’s tourism minister Daniela Santanchè has resigned in the wake of the defeat of Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni government in a referendum on judicial reforms

events.title: Lobsters and red carpet gowns: A new show celebrates Schiaparelli's surrealist fashion
events.description: The Victoria and Albert Museum is opening a new exhibition celebrating how the fashion house Schiaparelli turns fashion into surreal art

events.title: Nepal's newly elected parliament is sworn in months after a youth-led revolt
events.description: Nepal’s newly elected members of parliament have been sworn in, with nearly two-thirds of them from a political party that is less than four years old

events.title: Italy seizes millions in assets allegedly stolen from Bond co-star Ursula Andress
events.description: Italian police say they have seized about 20 million euros in property and assets linked to money stolen from Swiss actress and original Bond girl Ursula Andress

events.title: Militants ambush security forces in northern Nigeria, killing 9 soldiers and a police officer, authorities say
events.description: Militants ambush security forces in northern Nigeria, killing 9 soldiers and a police officer, authorities say

events.title: Cancer nurse turned archbishop celebrates election as first woman to lead Church of England
events.description: Sarah Mullally, a one-time cancer nurse who became a priest at the age of 40, will be installed as archbishop of Canterbury

events.title: BBC names ex-Google executive Matt Brittin its new director-general as it faces a feud with Trump
events.description: Former Google executive Matt Brittin has been announced as the new director-general of the BBC

events.title: Haitian gangs expand reach as police are accused of ‘summary executions’
events.description: Deadly gangs in Haiti are expanding their reach to include control over key sea and road routes as police in the beleaguered Caribbean island nation are being accused of using “unnecessary and disproportionate lethal force and summary executions.”

events.title: Harvard student says Jewish classmates feel 'unwelcome' as multibillion dollar DOJ lawsuit looms
events.description: A Harvard sophomore says Jewish students feel unwelcome as the Trump DOJ files a multibillion-dollar lawsuit over the school's handling of antisemitism.

events.title: Ex-Illinois governor Rod Blagojevich says Sheridan Gorman’s killing may have been a 'gang initiation'
events.description: Rod Blagojevich claims the illegal immigrant accused of killing Sheridan Gorman in Chicago did so as a gang initiation, alleging a political cover-up.

events.title: Michigan judge blasts woman for driving during Zoom court hearing: ‘Do you think I'm that stupid?’
events.description: A Michigan judge challenged a woman who joined a virtual hearing from a vehicle, questioning whether she was driving and ultimately issuing a default judgment.

events.title: More than 90% of Iranian missiles intercepted but a dangerous imbalance is emerging and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 More than 90% of Iranian missiles intercepted but a dangerous imbalance is emerging and more top headlines

events.title: Cop on trial denies gunning for fellow officer in clash at home; prosecutors argue lethal risk was real
events.description: Former North Andover Officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons testified she never aimed her gun at Officer Patrick Noonan, claiming she was attempting suicide when shot.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Cop on trial denies gunning for fellow officer in clash at home; prosecutors argue lethal risk was real

events.title: US Army raises enlistment age to 42 and eases marijuana policies to bolster ranks
events.description: The U.S. Army is raising its maximum recruitment age to 42 and removing restrictions for single marijuana convictions to boost technical talent.

events.title: Alleged predator accused of kidnapping UCLA students, sexual assault – police fear more victims
events.description: Alexander Schecter, 24, of Santa Monica is accused of rape, kidnapping and more after allegedly trapping two UCLA students in his car in early March.

events.title: EU blocks US vote to define gender as biological men and women at UN women’s forum
events.description: The EU used a procedural motion to block a U.S. resolution at the United Nations that sought to define gender as referring to biological men and women, leaving it without a vote.
